title: Add humanTree root history buffer matching agentTree's 30-root buffer

artifacts.description: Add ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 circular buffer for humanTree roots (humanRootHistory mapping + humanRootIndex counter), populate buffer on every human enrollment, update verifyHandshake to check humanMerkleRoot against buffer instead of exact current-root match
Extend interface to expose isKnownHumanRoot(bytes32 root) view function mirroring existing isKnownAgentRoot pattern
No circuit changes required — humanMerkleRoot is already a public output; the registry-side buffer check is purely in the verifier contract
Update Section on humanTree liveness: document ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 parity with agentTree, clarify that verifiers MUST accept any root within the rolling window
Add note under humanTree state transitions that enrollment does not immediately invalidate in-flight proofs due to 30-root history buffer

Tests: (1) proof with current root passes, (2) proof with root N-1 through N-29 passes after 30 new enrollments, (3) proof with root N-30 (evicted) fails, (4) circular wrap-around at slot 30, (5) isKnownHumanRoot view returns correct boolean for boundary cases
Expose isKnownHumanRoot helper in TS SDK client to allow off-chain staleness checks before submitting proofs
Short doc explaining the liveness guarantee, window size rationale, and how operators should size their proof submission timeout relative to expected enrollment rate

steps: Audit IdentityRegistry.sol agentTree buffer implementation (humanRootHistory mapping, ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE constant, circular index logic) to extract exact pattern for replication
Add uint256 public humanRootIndex and mapping(uint256 => bytes32) public humanRootHistory storage variables to IdentityRegistry alongside existing agentTree equivalents
In the human enrollment function (enrollHuman or equivalent), after updating the Merkle root, write the new root into humanRootHistory[humanRootIndex % ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE] and increment humanRootIndex

Add internal _isKnownHumanRoot(bytes32 root) view that iterates the circular buffer (up to ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE slots) and returns true if root matches any entry — mirror _isKnownAgentRoot pattern exactly
Add public isKnownHumanRoot(bytes32 root) external view function that wraps the internal helper, update IIdentityRegistry.sol interface
Update verifyHandshake: replace require(humanMerkleRoot == currentHumanRoot) with require(_isKnownHumanRoot(humanMerkleRoot), 'stale human root')

Write test file covering: current root accepted, roots 1–29 positions back accepted after N enrollments, root 30 positions back rejected, circular index wrap at slot boundary, isKnownHumanRoot view correctness
Run npm run test:contracts and confirm all existing tests still pass plus new buffer tests pass
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md: add humanTree liveness section documenting ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 parity, reference agentTree section for consistency
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md: note that humanTree state transitions preserve 30-root window
Expose isKnownHumanRoot in sdk/src/registry.ts TS client for off-chain pre-flight checks
Write docs/root-history-parity.md explaining liveness guarantee and operator guidance on proof submission timing
Run npm run compile:contracts to regenerate ABI artifacts, verify no breaking ABI changes to existing interface
Commit with DCO sign-off: git commit -s -m 'fix(registry): add 30-root history buffer for humanTree matching agentTree parity'
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol (existing agentTree ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 implementation as reference pattern)
contracts/src/IIdentityRegistry.sol (interface must be updated atomically with implementation)
sdk/src/registry.ts (TS SDK registry client for isKnownHumanRoot exposure)
